definitions: To frame or express (words, a concept, a problem, etc.) in a new or different way.
To put a new frame on (a picture, a window, etc.).
examples: She decided to reframe the problem to find a new solution.
They need to reframe the debate to include more perspectives.
He asked the artist to reframe the painting.
pastTenses: reframed (simple past), has/have reframed (present perfect), reframed (past perfect)
